top_weaknesses.issue: Too abstract — nouns stacked on nouns without one concrete multiplayer scene
top_weaknesses.fix: Open with one vivid workflow: a broker agent asks a carrier agent for a missing doc, learns the pattern, every later claim gets faster

top_weaknesses.issue: Mechanism of network effect is asserted, not shown — no explicit loop
top_weaknesses.fix: Name the loop tightly: more participants → more exception data → better resolution playbooks → lower onboarding cost → more participants

top_weaknesses.issue: List of verticals (insurance, freight, healthcare, legal) is breadth without depth
top_weaknesses.fix: Pick ONE vertical (say claims) and develop it; use the others as quick contrast or cut

top_weaknesses.issue: Citations feel bolted on (Bessemer, Salesforce, Menlo) rather than load-bearing
top_weaknesses.fix: Use each citation only where it sharpens a specific claim, not as credibility confetti

top_weaknesses.issue: VC-memo phrasing ('compounding workflow graph', 'workflow density across participants', 'shared operating memory')
top_weaknesses.fix: Translate abstract noun phrases into user-visible changes: fewer rejected forms, faster approvals, fewer manual escalations

top_weaknesses.issue: No named loser/enemy — who is on the wrong side of this thesis?
top_weaknesses.fix: Identify what loses if multiplayer wins: horizontal copilots, RPA, generic APIs, or single-tenant SaaS

consensus_summary: Strong contrarian thesis ('compounding workflow graph, not a viral loop') buried under abstraction. Pick one vertical, name the loop concretely, and add an enemy to push this from 7 to share-worthy.
